svg.text: A tidy argument collapses because one premise is unsupported even though the structure itself is valid.
Start with the concrete case: A tidy argument collapses because one premise is unsupported even though the structure itself is valid.
Name the hidden structure: Soundness checklist tying valid structure to true or well-supported premises.
Watch for the trap: Stopping analysis once the structure looks neat.
Transfer the habit immediately: Evaluate one real argument for soundness and identify its weakest premise.

Unlike broad intro courses, this lesson turns soundness into a practical operating skill through a real scenario, a visual model, a retrieval gate, and a transfer task built around argument mapping, evidence discipline, and fair-minded judgment.
Stopping analysis once the structure looks neat.

Evaluate one real argument for soundness and identify its weakest premise.
The goal is not to memorize the label, but to practice a repeatable move in real judgment. Ask the learner to produce a concrete artifact, not just an opinion.
